rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
The course aims at expanding students' proficiency in the four skills (Speaking - Listening - Reading - Writing), yet with a greater focus on oral communication.
Students will handle a variety of topics in various formats (short conversations, spoken pattern practice, watching and understanding videos on miscellaneous cultural topics, performing easy and short presentations on favorite topics, communicative games).
goals: Expanding students' proficiency in the four skills (Speaking - Listening - Reading - Writing), yet with a greater focus on oral communication;

Handling a variety of topics in various formats (short conversations, spoken pattern practice, watching and understanding videos on miscellaneous cultural topics, performing easy and short presentations on favorite topics, communicative games).

plan.text:
- Course Presentation (schedule; syllabus; instructor's policies);
- Getting to know "you";

- Speaking about one's personality;
→ What kind of person are you? What's unique about you? What are your tendencies and inclinations? Who do you get along with?

- Stereotypes and Images;
Speaking pattern & Conversation-based session
- My life as a Student: frustrations, satisfactions, lifestyle;

- Food;
→ one's addictions / one's eating habits

- What WOULD you do? What will you do?
→ Speaking about Unrealistic and Realistic scenarios, with a focus on grammatical structures;

- What would you do if you were Japan's Prime Minister / Legislator?
Group Presentation-based session
- Game time; → Tell the Truth/Tell a Lie; Pictionary;

- Travel Experiences;
→ Where have you been? What great memories have you made?
- Used to VS Be used to [continued];

Midterm Exam Session
→ with instructor interview format;

Midterm Exam Session [continued]
→ with instructor interview format;

- Strange Katakana Words!
→ How should we actually say this in proper English?

- Strange Katakana Words! [continued]
→ How should we actually say this in proper English?

- Expressing one's opinions, feelings and emotions;
→ Practice of miscellaneous preexisting patterns and formulas used to that end;

- Expressing one's opinions, feelings and emotions [continued];
→ Practice of miscellaneous preexisting patterns and formulas used to that end;
- My origins, my Family History, my hometown and its good and bad points;
- Phrasal Verbs Essentials [1]
- Phrasal Verbs Essentials [2]

- Education in Japan and in the rest of the World, a comparison;
Reading and Speaking-based session

- How can we improve education?
Short presentation-based session
- Game time, vocabulary brainstorming and miscellaneous;

- Future Plans, Career Goals, Personal Aspirations;
→ What are your goals and hopes for the/your future?

- Global issues / Wars / Environment
→ What can we possibly do to make the world a better place? How can I get involved?
Reading and Conversation-based session
- Review and Practice ahead of the Final Exam;

- Final Exam [1]
With instructor interview-based evaluation

- Final Exam [2]
With instructor interview-based evaluation

textbooks: All course materials will be provided by the teacher.
prereq: This class is available to all Lower and Upper Division Students. It is not a requirement for graduation.
prep:
Students will be required to review conversations along with the applied language techniques, functions and vocabulary studied
in class in order to reinforce their competence, prepare for future lessons, presentations and in‑class assessment.
teachers: ◎ ジョナサン ヘルムリンガー
